body:
## Review Summary

The toolkit structure is well-organized and the Makefile-based workflow is clean. Two categories of issues: (1) **documentation is wrong for `profile-sp1.sh`** — README.md and usage_guide.md both document the old signature (`<input_file> [output_dir] [sample_rate]`) but the script actually takes `<block_number> [output_dir] [rpc_url]`; and (2) **several files are inconsistent with the `.gitignore` setup** — the `profiles/` gitignore blocks everything but a 979-line profile dump was force-committed, and the `inputs/` and `benchmarks/` directories (created by the scripts) have no gitignore at all.

Also, `run-bench.sh` doesn't read the `$RPC_URL` environment variable as a fallback unlike all other scripts — see inline comment.

comments.body:
This gitignore blocks all files in `profiles/`, but the PR includes `profiles/zisk/encodedtrie_optimizations/05_avoid_nodetype_clone.txt` (979 lines, force-added). That file also leaks a local path (`/Users/xqft/git/ethrex/...`) on its first line.

Either remove the committed profile (it contradicts the intent here) or restructure the gitignore to explicitly allow reference profiles in a subdirectory.

Also, the `inputs/` and `benchmarks/` directories are created by the scripts but have no gitignore — binary `.bin` files and benchmark logs will show up in `git status` and could be accidentally committed.

Wrong signature documented. The actual `profile-sp1.sh` takes:
```
bin/profile-sp1.sh <block_number> [output_dir] [rpc_url] [description]
```
Not `<input_file> [output_dir] [sample_rate] [description]`. The script's own `Usage:` message (line 22 of `profile-sp1.sh`) correctly says `<block_number>`.
Same issue as in README.md — the actual script signature is `<block_number> [output_dir] [rpc_url] [description]`, not `<input_file> [output_dir] [sample_rate] [description]`.

If `ethrex-replay` ever changes its output filename convention, `$TEMP_OUTPUT` won't match, the `if [ -f ... ]` silently does nothing, and the script prints "Input generated: $OUTPUT_FILE" even though the file doesn't exist.

Suggestion: fail explicitly when the rename source is missing:
```bash
if [ -f "$TEMP_OUTPUT" ]; then
    mv "$TEMP_OUTPUT" "$OUTPUT_FILE"
else
    echo "Error: Expected output not found: $TEMP_OUTPUT"
    echo "ethrex-replay may have changed its output naming."
    exit 1
fi
```

Unlike `generate-input.sh` and `profile-sp1.sh`, this doesn't fall back to the `$RPC_URL` environment variable when the positional arg is omitted:
```bash
# This script:
RPC_URL=${3:-"http://localhost:8545"}

# Other scripts:
RPC_URL=${3:-"${RPC_URL:-http://localhost:8545}"}
```

`export RPC_URL=...` won't work with this script unless explicitly passed as arg 3.
